fact: Ringling Brothers waterproofed their big top with paraffin dissolved in gasoline
source: The Circus Fire: A True Story of an American Tragedy, Stewart O'Nan (2000)

fact: Six thousand people were seated under the tent
source: The Circus Fire: A True Story of an American Tragedy, Stewart O'Nan (2000) — estimates range from 6,000 to 8,000 attendees

fact: Exits were blocked by narrow animal chutes and steel runways/cages
source: The Circus Fire: A True Story of an American Tragedy, Stewart O'Nan (2000)

fact: The paraffin-coated canvas melted rather than simply burning, dripping liquid fire onto spectators
source: The Circus Fire, Stewart O'Nan (2000); also documented in Hartford Fire Marshal investigation records
Connecticut State Library — Hartford Circus Fire, July 6, 1944. The official death toll is 167 (some sources cite 168 or 169 depending on later deaths).
